title: Senior Backend Engineer, Cloud Platform
description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>As a&nbsp;Senior Backend Engineer&nbsp;on the&nbsp;Cloud Platform team, you will play a key role in building the core systems and services that power Postman’s internal platform. You’ll help create new backend services that manage how we deploy, scale, and operate our infrastructure and product services, leveraging&nbsp;Java, Spring Boot, and Hibernate (JPA)&nbsp;on top of&nbsp;cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes, ArgoCD, Istio, and Terraform.</p> <p>This role is highly impactful: the systems you build will be used across Postman engineering, enabling faster delivery, better scalability, and a stronger developer experience. You’ll also have the opportunity to contribute to open source, shaping tools that extend beyond Postman’s boundaries.</p> <h2>What You’ll Do</h2> <ul> <li>Design and develop backend services in Java and Spring Boot to support Postman’s internal Cloud Platform.<br><br></li> <li>Architect new services that manage service deployment, lifecycle, and scaling across Kubernetes clusters.<br><br></li> <li>Implement GitOps workflows (ArgoCD) to support continuous delivery.<br><br></li> <li>Integrate with cloud-native tooling such as Istio, Helm, and Terraform.<br><br></li> <li>Apply strong software engineering practices: testing, code reviews, CI/CD, and documentation.<br><br></li> <li>Drive innovation by contributing to open-source projects where applicable.<br><br></li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with Cloud Architects, DevOps engineers, and product teams to align solutions with business needs.<br><br></li> <li>Ensure performance, reliability, and scalability of services that run at Postman’s scale.</li> </ul> <h2>About You</h2> <ul> <li>5+ years of professional experience in backend software development.</li> <li> <p>Strong expertise in Java, Spring Boot, and Hibernate (JPA).</p> </li> <li> <p>Proven experience designing and maintaining APIs and distributed backend services.</p> </li> <li> <p>Familiarity with Kubernetes, Argo, Istio, and Terraform.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong understanding of software design principles (scalability, extensibility, resilience).</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience with build and testing tools (Gradle, Maven, JUnit).</p> </li> <li> <p>Proficiency with Git and Gitflow workflows.</p> </li> <li> <p>Excellent collaboration and communication skills.</p> </li> <li>Strong security mindset and awareness of compliance frameworks.</li> </ul> <p>The reasonably estimated base salary for this role ranges from $210,000 to $240,000, plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.</p> <h2 data-renderer-start-pos="3620"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Our Values</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="3633">At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.</p> <h2><strong>Equal opportunity</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.</p></div>

title: Senior Fullstack Software Engineer - (Customer Journey)
description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>The Customer Journey team at Postman is responsible for shaping how users experience the product from their first interaction through to becoming active, engaged customers.&nbsp;</p> <p>We own the critical paths between sign-up and purchase including new user onboarding and the moments where users discover and adopt key features. As Postman expands what it means to be a customer, from enterprise organizations to teams of engineers to individual developers working with AI agents, this team plays a central role in defining and evolving those experiences.&nbsp;</p> <p>We’re looking for a Senior Engineer who is excited to build and improve product journeys end-to-end. You’ll work at the intersection of product, design, and engineering to create intuitive, high-quality experiences that help users find value in Postman—whether they’re exploring on their own or collaborating as part of a team.</p> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do&nbsp;</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Own and improve key product journeys from sign-up through onboarding, feature discovery, and purchase&nbsp;</li> <li>Build and refine user experiences that help developers and teams quickly understand and realize the value of Postman&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and implement features that drive adoption of core platform capabilities across different user types (individuals, teams, and AI-assisted workflows)&nbsp;</li> <li>Partner closely with Product, Design, and Data to identify opportunities and prioritize improvements across the user journey&nbsp;</li> <li>Use experimentation and analytics to inform decisions and validate the impact of changes&nbsp;</li> <li>Develop a deep understanding of how developers use Postman—individually and collaboratively—and apply those insights to improve the product&nbsp;</li> <li>Iterate quickly, delivering thoughtful, incremental improvements that compound over time</li> <li>Contribute to the evolution of our onboarding and discovery systems as the product and customer base continue to grow&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h2><strong>About You&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p><strong>Must have:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>8+ years of experience building frontend or full-stack applications with a focus on product and user experience&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong fundamentals in JavaScript, HTML, and CSS; experience with modern frameworks like React or Next.js</li> <li>Track record of building and improving product experiences that drive user engagement and adoption&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience using data, analytics, or experimentation to guide product decisions</li> <li>Strong product sense and attention to detail—you care deeply about how things work and feel&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to take ownership of ambiguous problem spaces and drive them to clear outcomes&nbsp;</li> <li>Excellent communication skills and experience collaborating across functions</li> <li>Curiosity about how developers work, and a desire to build tools that make them more effective&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice-to-have:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with full-stack JavaScript / TypeScript&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with state management tools like MobX&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience working on B2B products, developer tools, or AI-powered experiences</li> <li>Experience working in a fast-paced, startup environment&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>This role is based in the greater San Francisco area, and the reasonably estimated salary for this role ranges from $210,000 to $240,000 plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.</p> <h2 data-renderer-start-pos="3620"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Our Values</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="3633">At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.</p> <h2><strong>Equal opportunity</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.</p></div>

You'll establish engineering excellence through improved architecture, performance optimization, and maintainable code patterns that ensure our platform delivers exceptional experiences.</p> <p>You'll work alongside other senior engineers to own different aspects of our UI Platform (design system, accessibility, internationalization, theming, etc.). This is a unique opportunity to have an immediate impact on the foundation that hundreds of Postman engineers and our 40+ million customers rely on daily.</p> <h2 id="m_3123524841401721341gmail-What-You'll-Do"><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <p>The Client Platform team is a global engineering organization responsible for creating a "paved path" for all Postmanauts to develop and release web-based applications. Our platform is the foundation that 240+ engineers at Postman rely on daily to deliver new features and improvements to over 40 million customers.</p> <ul> <li> <p>Design and implement scalable, maintainable front-end solutions, focused on the presentation layer</p> <ul> <li> <p>Design system and component library</p> </li> <li> <p>Application shell &amp; layout</p> </li> <li> <p>Specialized UI components (code editor, text editor, data grid)</p> </li> <li> <p>Global UI patterns</p> </li> <li> <p>Accessibility</p> </li> <li> <p>Internationalization/localization</p> </li> <li> <p>Theming/styling architecture</p> </li> <li> <p>Keyboard shortcuts</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li> <p>Own technical initiatives end-to-end from design through delivery and maintenance</p> </li> <li> <p>Establish best practices and standards for SDK development</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure SDK meets product requirements</p> </li> <li> <p>Mentor other engineers and contribute to technical decision-making</p> </li> <li> <p>Drive technical initiatives that enhance the SDK's performance, reliability, and developer experience</p> </li> <li> <p>Identify and proactively address technical debt and opportunities for improvement in our platform</p> </li> <li> <p>Participate in production support and incident resolution for SDK-related issues</p> </li> </ul> <h2><strong>About You</strong></h2> <h3 id="m_3123524841401721341gmail-Experience-Requirements"><span style="font-size: small;">Experience Requirements</span></h3> <ul> <li> <p>5-10 years of professional software engineering experience</p> </li> <li> <p>2+ years in a similar senior role</p> </li> <li> <p>Deep understanding of modern web practices and associated technologies (JavaScript, TypeScript, React, etc.)</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience with design systems, accessibility compliance (WCAG&nbsp;2.1&nbsp;AA), or internationalization at scale</p> </li> </ul> <h3 id="m_3123524841401721341gmail-You-Are-Someone-Who"><span style="font-size: small;">You Are Someone Who</span></h3> <ul> <li> <p>Communicates clearly and listens effectively. You seek new perspectives and build trust with your colleagues.</p> </li> <li> <p>Loves the challenge of constraints and excels at finding creative solutions.</p> </li> <li> <p>Takes ownership of problems, not solutions, and celebrates success as a team.</p> </li> <li> <p>Has demonstrated expertise in code quality, performance optimization, and engineering excellence.</p> </li> <li> <p>Has experience designing and implementing maintainable, scalable frontend architectures for complex applications, including component libraries and SDKs that can be effectively reused across multiple systems.</p> </li> <li> <p>Has a proven track record of raising the quality bar in previous roles through both technical solutions and positive cultural impact.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 id="m_3123524841401721341gmail-Nice-to-have"><span style="font-size: small;">Nice-to-have</span></h3> <ul> <li> <p>Experience with cross-platform applications (Electron or similar) and understanding the unique challenges of desktop/web hybrid environments.</p> </li> <li> <p>Creating widely-adopted (open source) libraries/SDKs with 1000+ active developers.</p> </li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. title: Senior Software Engineer Manager - Ecosystems
location: Cupertino, California, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>Postman is seeking a strategic and results-driven engineering leader who is passionate about APIs, partnerships, ecosystems, collaboration, and developer success.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>As the Sr. Engineering Manager for Ecosystem, you’ll build and lead a talented group of engineers, fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and excellence while guiding the team to build scalable, reliable, high-performance features that power the future of our Ecosystem. You’ll work closely with other engineering managers, product managers, and designers to drive the vision and execution of the product roadmap that will empower developers + partners to create rich, integrated experiences that will help teams create, collaborate, manage, and scale their APIs and solutions.&nbsp; This position requires a strategic thinker with a deep understanding of developer ecosystems and a passion for delivering exceptional developer experiences.&nbsp; You will define our technical strategy to achieve our ecosystem goals, including how extensibility, integration frameworks and public APIs work at Postman, how integrations look, feel, and behave to our end users, and how customers discover, install, and buy apps via a marketplace.&nbsp;</p> <p>If you’re passionate about building products that make an impact, have a proven track record of leading engineering teams to success, and thrive in dynamic, fast-paced environments, we want you on our team!</p> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <h3>Team Leadership &amp; Development</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Hire, manage, mentor, and coach several teams of engineers, helping them grow both technically and professionally.</p> </li> <li> <p>Drive and uphold a culture of respect, integrity, inclusion, ownership, and accountability within the team.</p> </li> <li> <p>Set clear goals and provide regular feedback to ensure your team is motivated and aligned with the product and company vision.</p> </li> <li> <p>Design strategies for developer success across many areas, including those that are still in the early stages of finding their product-market fit.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experiment, design, and influence rigorously the creation of new kinds of experiences for consumers and the new business models that will support builders, creators, partners, and developers.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>Technical Leadership</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Facilitate and coordinate the technical design and execution of complex features for Postman’s ecosystem platform ensuring scalability, performance, and reliability for millions of customers and partners everywhere.</p> </li> <li> <p>Partner with leaders at strategic companies in the ecosystem to define high value product offerings through Postman app as well as the partner app and marketplaces.</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product managers, designers, and other engineering managers, to deliver high-quality, customer-focused features.</p> </li> <li> <p>Ensure engineering best practices are upheld, including code reviews, testing, and continuous delivery.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>Project Management</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Own the engineering roadmap for Ecosystem, including Developer Tooling, Strategic Integrations and Marketplace, balancing short-term delivery with long-term technical and&nbsp; business investments.</p> </li> <li> <p>Break down complex initiatives into clear, actionable milestones and manage the delivery of these projects on time.</p> </li> <li> <p>Proactively identify and resolve roadblocks, ensuring your team meets its deadlines and delivers features with high quality.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>Collaboration &amp; Communication</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Work closely with stakeholders across engineering, product, and design teams to ensure alignment on priorities and deliverables.</p> </li> <li> <p>Foster open communication within the team, promoting collaboration and transparency.</p> </li> <li> <p>Represent the engineering team in meetings with leadership, advocating for technical needs and constraints while aligning with broader company goals.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>Operational Excellence</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Ensure the reliability and performance of Ecosystem products by driving operational best practices for monitoring, logging, and incident response.</p> </li> <li> <p>Optimize the team’s workflow by implementing automation and improving processes where needed.</p> </li> <li> <p>Maintain a high level of code quality and system robustness by promoting test-driven development and continuous integration.</p> </li> </ul> <h2><strong>About You</strong></h2> <p>You are a seasoned engineering leader with a passion for building great products, fostering strong teams, and solving challenging technical problems. You have a track record of managing high-performing engineering teams and driving technical projects from concept to delivery geared towards helping 3rd party developers build impactful apps quickly and successfully (ex. Shopify, Atlassian, HubSpot, Slack, etc.)</p> <h3>Must Have Qualifications:</h3> <ul> <li> <p>10+ years of experience as a software engineer, with 5+ years in an engineering management or leadership role.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience building and launching 0 → 1 ecosystem features, platform extensibility (UI components, frameworks, APIs), developer tooling and/or products for external technical audiences.</p> </li> <li> <p>Proven experience leading teams in fast-paced, agile environments.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong background in backend or frontend development (experience with React, Java a plus).</p> </li> <li> <p>Demonstrated ability to coach and mentor engineers, helping them grow and succeed.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience managing the full software development lifecycle, from design through production support.</p> </li> <li> <p>Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>Nice-to-Have:</h3> <ul> <li> <p>Familiarity with API development, cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure), and container orchestration (Kubernetes).</p> </li> <li> <p>Prior experience working in a SaaS or cloud-based environment.</p> </li> <li> <p>A passion for building systems that prioritize scalability, performance, and operational excellence.</p> </li> </ul> <p>The reasonably estimated base salary for this role ranges from $260,000 to $300,000, plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. title: Senior Software Engineer, Monetization
description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2>The Opportunity</h2> <p>The mission of the Monetization team is to establish our product-led packaging, subscription model, and billing and scale them as we expand and grow our business.</p> <p>Our team is responsible for building robust Billing systems, focusing on payments, renewals, sales tax, and revenue. We also work closely with all the teams to integrate their products with our packaging model thus offering our customers the best features, pricing, and services through self-serve purchases and our Sales channels.</p> <p>We’re looking for an experienced Senior Engineer who will lead our initiatives for a powerful config-driven platform, improving customer purchase experience and working on the internal tools to match the scale of the growth of Postman.</p> <h2>What You'll Do</h2> <ul> <li>Work on our core Monetization platform to improve offer management, including packaging, plans and pricing, which support the pre-purchase workflow and on our Billing platform to improve data capture for revenue recognition and ARR reporting.</li> <li>Lead the active development of our internal services to introduce new frameworks, optimize the APIs, and improve scale and reliability.</li> <li>Contribute to developing APIs and products while working with a cross-functional team of Product Managers, Designers, Support and Product, Quality, Security and Platform Engineers.</li> <li>Perform code reviews and advocate better practices for software engineering by mentoring and encouraging others around you to build a quality product.</li> <li>Utilize your technical and domain expertise to help architect the solution and also help in cross-squad knowledge sharing on best practices.</li> <li>Design &amp; build any additional internal tooling required to ease the development &amp; maintenance of the product.</li> </ul> <h2>About You</h2> <ul> <li>6+ years of experience developing complex, distributed software applications at Postman’s scale.</li> <li>Proficient with Javascript, Node.js, TypeScript, Mysql, Redis, and essential understanding of ReactJS and web fundamentals.</li> <li>Strong knowledge of Database fundamentals, especially on the performance aspects such as optimizing queries, schema design, indexing, and caching.</li> <li>Strong Infrastructure knowledge, including AWS systems, Kubernetes, CI/CD Pipelines, Automation scripts, and monitoring tools (like Cloudwatch, and Newrelic).</li> <li>Proven record of building products and services from scratch and leading teams for new and complex projects.</li> <li>Prior experience in closely collaborating with and respectfully challenging other stakeholders, such as PM, EM, etc.</li> <li>(Bonus) Good understanding of Monetization platforms (Payments, Subscriptions, Taxes, Revenue recognition, Sales Driven purchase contracts, and custom subscriptions).</li> <li>You enjoy learning and sharing knowledge within and across teams.</li> <li>Sense of humor</li> </ul> <p>The reasonably estimated base salary for this role ranges from $200,000 - $240,000 plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.</p> <h2 data-renderer-start-pos="3620"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Our Values</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="3633">At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.</p> <h2><strong>Equal opportunity</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.</p></div>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Postman Insights
description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2>The <strong>Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>With <a href="https://www.postman.com/product/postman-insights/">Postman Insights</a>, our vision is to help all developers more quickly find and fix production issues. Our vision is to provide productive dashboards within 15 minutes of a developer signing up for an account. With our first product, we aim to provide the fastest, easiest way to find and fix failing APIs.</p> <p>We are looking for a high-ownership, product-oriented infrastructure engineer to help us scale our product to bigger users. Postman Insights works by watching API traffic at the network level, in real-time. You’ll get to work on exciting scaling problems, balancing long-term infrastructure goals with short-to-medium term product needs.</p> <p><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OHMxgRuW9H8&amp;t=3370s">Watch our launch demo</a> from POST/CON.</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Design, build, and scale our backend infrastructure to process time-series network traffic, at large scale, in real-time.</li> <li>Build and maintain AI algorithms to analyze time-series network data to improve API discovery, metrics, and more.</li> <li>Take ownership of the engineering roadmap for the backend infrastructure of Postman Insights, setting the technical direction and priorities.</li> <li>Collaborate with documentation and marketing teams to help explain the capabilities and limitations that the infrastructure unlocks.</li> <li>Partner with the broader team to make design and architecture decisions for Postman Insights.</li> <li>Support the engineering manager in growing the team by contributing to technical planning, onboarding new members, and participating in hiring efforts.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>About you</strong></h2> <p>You’re an engineer who thrives on diving into the unknown, collaborating with others, and tackling challenging, complex problems with a can-do attitude. You have a passion for delivering seamless and delightful experiences to users, no matter how intricate the technical hurdles may be.</p> <p>As one of the first dedicated engineers in the platform area for Postman Insights, you’ll play a critical role in shaping the foundation of this team. Your work will not only directly impact the success of our platform but also set the stage for expanding the team and scaling our capabilities in this space.</p> <h3>Previous Experience</h3> <p>Must have:</p> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience shipping production-grade software.</li> <li>Experience scaling large-scale software systems.</li> <li>Proven ability to work independently, with at least two significant projects where you were either the primary contributor delivering substantial portions of the work and/or led a team of engineers to successfully execute the project.</li> <li>Strong communication skills, with a track record of aligning cross-functional stakeholders and unblocking challenges to meet business objectives.</li> <li>Based in the San Francisco Bay Area.</li> </ul> <p>Nice-to-have:</p> <ul> <li>Experience working on an early-stage product.</li> <li>Experience working with time-series data.</li> <li>Experience collaborating with distributed teams across multiple time zones.</li> </ul> <p>The reasonably estimated base salary for this role ranges from $210,000 to 240,000, plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.</p> <h2 data-renderer-start-pos="3620"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Our Values</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="3633">At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. title: Senior Software Engineer, Backend (Product Engineering)
location: New York, New York, United States
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the intelligent finance platform that enables companies to spend smarter and move faster in more than 200 markets. By combining global corporate cards and banking with intuitive spend management, bill pay, and travel software, Brex enables founders and finance teams to accelerate operations, gain real-time visibility, and control spend effortlessly. Brex’s AI-native automation and world-class service eliminate manual expense and accounting tasks for customers so they can focus on what matters most. Tens of thousands of the world's best companies run on Brex, including DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, and SeatGeek.</p> <p>Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. We’re committed to building a diverse team and inclusive culture and believe your potential should only be limited by how big you can dream. We make this a reality by empowering you with the tools, resources, and support you need to grow your career.</p></div><p><strong>Engineering at Brex</strong></p> <p>Engineering at Brex is about building systems that scale with speed and intention. Our teams span Software, Data, Security, and IT, and operate with high autonomy and deep collaboration. We tackle hard technical problems, own our outcomes, and push for excellence at every level — from architecture to deployment. It’s an environment where engineering is a craft, and builders become leaders.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do</strong></p> <p>As an engineer at Brex, you will help develop new products from concept to launch, whether for a 5-person startup or a 5,000-person-strong enterprise. We’re looking for folks with an interest in building products and tools, and who are comfortable in dealing with lots of moving pieces. You’ll be building customer-facing products that will drive direct business impact and shape our long-term technical vision with a high-quality bar. You'll have the opportunity to learn and push the frontier of providing the best financial software experience to help companies grow. You’ll be encouraged to be metric and data-driven and to think creatively to help Brex scale and get prepared for the new markets we are about to enter.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Where you’ll work</strong></p> <p>This role will be based in our New York office. We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week, Monday, Wednesday and Thursday. As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Collaborate with engineers across the company to build new features and products end-to-end.</li> <li>Work alongside user-facing teams like Sales and Support to deeply understand our customers' needs.</li> <li>Design and implement solutions to improve our customers' banking experiences.</li> <li>Own problems end-to-end, from user experience and data models to scalability, operability, and ongoing metrics.</li> <li>Tune and polish features to a high degree of excellence.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of professional experience designing, developing, and deploying applications with a focus on backend.</li> <li>Experience building and designing scalable backend systems</li> <li>Experience working with SQL or NoSQL databases</li> <li>Experience working with backend programming languages (Java, Kotlin, Python)</li> <li>Familiarity with software engineering development cycles</li> <li>Ability to hold yourself and the team to high standards</li> <li>Strong communication and interpersonal skills</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation:</strong></p> <p>The expected salary range for this role is $192,000-$240,000. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity. Depending on the position offered, equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package.</p> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Brex recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with a&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://brex.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://brex.com" data-sk="tooltip_parent">brex.com</a>&nbsp;domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Brex via other sources should be ignored.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Frontend
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the intelligent finance platform that enables companies to spend smarter and move faster in more than 200 markets. By combining global corporate cards and banking with intuitive spend management, bill pay, and travel software, Brex enables founders and finance teams to accelerate operations, gain real-time visibility, and control spend effortlessly. Brex’s AI-native automation and world-class service eliminate manual expense and accounting tasks for customers so they can focus on what matters most. Tens of thousands of the world's best companies run on Brex, including DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, and SeatGeek.</p> <p>Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. We’re committed to building a diverse team and inclusive culture and believe your potential should only be limited by how big you can dream. We make this a reality by empowering you with the tools, resources, and support you need to grow your career.</p></div><p><strong>Engineering at Brex</strong></p> <p>Engineering at Brex is about building systems that scale with speed and intention. Our teams span Software, Data, Security, and IT, and operate with high autonomy and deep collaboration. We tackle hard technical problems, own our outcomes, and push for excellence at every level - from architecture to deployment. It’s an environment where engineering is a craft, and builders become leaders.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do</strong></p> <p>As an Senior Frontend Engineer, you will be working across the entire Brex stack, creating and maintaining new products, features, and building tools to help scale Brex. You will have the opportunity to learn and push the frontier of providing the best financial software experience to help companies grow. You’ll be encouraged to be metric and data-driven and to think creatively to help Brex scale and get prepared for the new markets we are about to enter. We’re looking for folks with not only an interest in building products and tools but also interested in leading multi-functional projects with lots of moving pieces. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to drive direct business impact and shape our long-term technical vision with a high-quality bar.</p> <p><strong>Where you’ll work</strong></p> <p>This role will be based in our Seattle office. We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week, Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Work with engineers across the company to build new features and products.</li> <li>Work side-by-side with user facing teams (Sales, Support) to best understand the needs of our customers.&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and implement experiments to improve our customer’s experiences.</li> <li>Own problems end-to-end, thinking through everything from user experience, data models, scalability, operability and ongoing metrics.</li> <li>Tune and polish features to a high-degree of excellence.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of professional experience with&nbsp;modern frontend technologies and tools like ES6+, React, TypeScript, and Webpack</li> <li>Experience architecting, developing, and testing client-side code end to end</li> <li>Experience developing client facing software products&nbsp;</li> <li>Uphold our high engineering standards</li> <li>Strong communication and interpersonal skills.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus points</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience collaborating with specialists in product, design, and operations</li> <li>Experience with developing in React Native for mobile</li> <li>Basic experience with design tools</li> <li>Great writing skills</li> <li>Proactive approach.</li> </ul> <p><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Compensation</strong></p> <p>The expected salary range for this role is $192,000 - $240,000 + equity. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity.&nbsp;</p> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. title: Senior Software Engineer, Full Stack
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the intelligent finance platform that enables companies to spend smarter and move faster in more than 200 markets. By combining global corporate cards and banking with intuitive spend management, bill pay, and travel software, Brex enables founders and finance teams to accelerate operations, gain real-time visibility, and control spend effortlessly. Brex’s AI-native automation and world-class service eliminate manual expense and accounting tasks for customers so they can focus on what matters most. Tens of thousands of the world's best companies run on Brex, including DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, and SeatGeek.</p> <p>Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. We’re committed to building a diverse team and inclusive culture and believe your potential should only be limited by how big you can dream. We make this a reality by empowering you with the tools, resources, and support you need to grow your career.</p></div><p><strong>Engineering at Brex</strong></p> <p>Engineering at Brex is about building systems that scale with speed and intention. Our teams span Software, Data, Security, and IT, and operate with high autonomy and deep collaboration. We tackle hard technical problems, own our outcomes, and push for excellence at every level — from architecture to deployment. It’s an environment where engineering is a craft, and builders become leaders.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do</strong></p> <p>As a Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer on our Task Workflows Platform team, you will build and scale the foundational infrastructure that powers core experiences across Brex. You will work across our extensive suite of platforms - including our multi-channel notifications engine, collaborative commenting system, and dynamic workflow rule builder. You will also play a pivotal role in evolving our task orchestration infrastructure infrastructure, helping us transition from a centralized, generic experience into highly tailored, purpose-built solutions for specific product domains.</p> <p>You will tackle complex full-stack challenges, from designing scalable backend APIs and rule-evaluation engines for custom automations, to crafting intuitive frontend UX for our domain-specific task interfaces and workflow builders. Beyond core platform work, you will be part of a team that directly shapes broader product experiences - including leveraging AI to streamline user workflows and supporting the continuous evolution of our productivity tools in web and mobile.</p> <p><strong>Where you’ll work</strong></p> <p>This role will be based in&nbsp;Vancouver office. We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday. As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Work with engineers across the company to build new features and products end-to-end</li> <li>Own problems end-to-end, thinking through everything from user experience, data models, scalability, operability and ongoing metrics.</li> <li>Provide technical leadership to team by driving roadmap direction, architectural design and mentorship</li> <li>Work side-by-side with user-facing teams (Sales, Support) to best understand the needs of our customers.</li> <li>Tune and polish features to a high degree of excellence.</li> <li>Identify and implement reliability and performance improvements</li> <li>Uphold our high engineering standards and bring consistency to the codebases, infrastructure, and processes you will encounter</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of professional experience designing, developing, and deploying full-stack products</li> <li>Strong proficiency in backend programming languages (Java, Kotlin, Python)</li> <li>Experience architecting, building, and maintaining scalable, high-availability distributed systems</li> <li>Experience designing and optimizing SQL and/or NoSQL databases, including data modeling, query performance tuning, and schema design</li> <li>Experience building and maintaining RESTful APIs and/or GraphQL services</li> <li>Experience with modern frontend technologies and tools like ES6+, React, TypeScript, and Webpack</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus points</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders and specialists in product, design, and operations</li> <li>Experience driving initiatives at a broader level across an organization or company</li> <li>Experience running in-product experiments, A/B testing</li> <li>Experience and interest in leveraging AI developer tools</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p>The expected salary range for this role is $192,000 - $240,000 CAD. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors, including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity. Depending on the position offered, equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package.</p> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Brex recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with a&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://brex.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://brex.com" data-sk="tooltip_parent">brex.com</a>&nbsp;domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Brex via other sources should be ignored.</em></p></div>

We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday. As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Work with engineers across the company to build new features and products end-to-end</li> <li>Own problems end-to-end, thinking through everything from user experience, data models, scalability, operability and ongoing metrics.</li> <li>Provide technical leadership to team by driving roadmap direction, architectural design and mentorship</li> <li>Work side-by-side with user-facing teams (Sales, Support) to best understand the needs of our customers.</li> <li>Tune and polish features to a high degree of excellence.</li> <li>Identify and implement reliability and performance improvements</li> <li>Uphold our high engineering standards and bring consistency to the codebases, infrastructure, and processes you will encounter</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of professional experience designing, developing, and deploying full-stack products</li> <li>Strong proficiency in backend programming languages (Java, Kotlin, Python)</li> <li>Experience architecting, building, and maintaining scalable, high-availability distributed systems</li> <li>Experience designing and optimizing SQL and/or NoSQL databases, including data modeling, query performance tuning, and schema design</li> <li>Experience building and maintaining RESTful APIs and/or GraphQL services</li> <li>Experience with modern frontend technologies and tools like ES6+, React, TypeScript, and Webpack</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus points</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders and specialists in product, design, and operations</li> <li>Experience driving initiatives at a broader level across an organization or company</li> <li>Experience running in-product experiments, A/B testing</li> <li>Experience and interest in leveraging AI developer tools</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p>The expected salary range for this role is $192,000 - $240,000. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors, including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity. Depending on the position offered, equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package.</p> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Brex recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with a&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://brex.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://brex.com" data-sk="tooltip_parent">brex.com</a>&nbsp;domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Brex via other sources should be ignored.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Product Data Platform
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the intelligent finance platform that enables companies to spend smarter and move faster in more than 200 markets. By combining global corporate cards and banking with intuitive spend management, bill pay, and travel software, Brex enables founders and finance teams to accelerate operations, gain real-time visibility, and control spend effortlessly. Brex’s AI-native automation and world-class service eliminate manual expense and accounting tasks for customers so they can focus on what matters most. Tens of thousands of the world's best companies run on Brex, including DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, and SeatGeek.</p> <p>Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. We’re committed to building a diverse team and inclusive culture and believe your potential should only be limited by how big you can dream. We make this a reality by empowering you with the tools, resources, and support you need to grow your career.</p></div><p><strong>Engineering at Brex</strong></p> <p>Engineering at Brex is about building systems that scale with speed and intention. Our teams span Software, Data, Security, and IT, and operate with high autonomy and deep collaboration. We tackle hard technical problems, own our outcomes, and push for excellence at every level — from architecture to deployment. It’s an environment where engineering is a craft, and builders become leaders.</p> <p><strong>Where you’ll work</strong></p> <p>This role will be based in our Seattle Office. We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week, Monday, Wednesday and Thursday.</p> <p>As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do</strong></p> <p>This opportunity is on our Product Data Platform (PDP) team, which builds and operates the high-performance data, query, and search infrastructure that powers critical Brex product experiences. PDP’s mission is to make Brex customizable, scalable, and reliable for finance teams, and that requires deeply optimized, production-grade backend systems.</p> <p>As a Senior Software Engineer, you’ll work on data-intensive, distributed systems at scale. You’ll lead architectural and performance improvements across the platform, driving better latency, correctness, and scalability across multiple product surfaces.&nbsp;PDP also builds AI-powered data products, including an agentic reporting experience that enables customers to interact with their financial data in dynamic, intelligent ways. Your work ensures these systems are fast, reliable, and built to scale.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Analyze and optimize complex query plans, execution paths, cost models, joins, and indexing strategies.</li> <li>Drive schema and access-pattern improvements to resolve systemic performance bottlenecks.</li> <li>Debug and remediate P95/P99 latency issues under load in production systems.</li> <li>Design and operate distributed systems with thoughtful tradeoffs around consistency, latency, caching, and failure modes.</li> <li>Evaluate existing architectures to proactively identify scaling risks and long-term reliability gaps.</li> <li>Implement and improve caching strategies, read/write separation, and replica usage.</li> <li>Contribute to and improve backend systems primarily in the JVM ecosystem (Kotlin/Java).</li> <li>Raise the technical bar through thoughtful design reviews and clear communication of tradeoffs.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of backend engineering experience building and operating production systems at scale.</li> <li>Experience building platforms or infrastructure used by real customers.</li> <li>Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally.</li> <li>Deep hands-on experience with relational databases (Postgres or Aurora strongly preferred)</li> <li>Strong expertise in query plan design and analysis, indexing strategies, and real-world database optimization.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hands-on experience with OpenSearch / Elasticsearch or similar systems in production</li> <li>Experience with multi-tenant or data-heavy platforms.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation:</strong></p> <ul> <li>The expected salary range for this role is $192,000 - $240,000. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity.</li> </ul> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Brex recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with a&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://brex.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://brex.com" data-sk="tooltip_parent">brex.com</a>&nbsp;domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Brex via other sources should be ignored.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Release Infra
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the intelligent finance platform that enables companies to spend smarter and move faster in more than 200 markets. By combining global corporate cards and banking with intuitive spend management, bill pay, and travel software, Brex enables founders and finance teams to accelerate operations, gain real-time visibility, and control spend effortlessly. Brex’s AI-native automation and world-class service eliminate manual expense and accounting tasks for customers so they can focus on what matters most. Tens of thousands of the world's best companies run on Brex, including DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, and SeatGeek.</p> <p>Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. We’re committed to building a diverse team and inclusive culture and believe your potential should only be limited by how big you can dream. We make this a reality by empowering you with the tools, resources, and support you need to grow your career.</p></div><p><strong>Engineering at Brex</strong></p> <p>Engineering at Brex is about building systems that scale with speed and intention. Our teams span Software, Data, Security, and IT, and operate with high autonomy and deep collaboration. We tackle hard technical problems, own our outcomes, and push for excellence at every level — from architecture to deployment. It’s an environment where engineering is a craft, and builders become leaders.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do</strong></p> <p>As a Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure (Release Engineering) at Brex, you will design, build, and operate the core systems that power Brex’s release, observability, and incident management processes. You will partner closely with product, platform, and operations teams to ensure releases are safe, fast, and reliable, and that our infrastructure scales securely as Brex grows.</p> <p><strong>Where you’ll work</strong></p> <p>This role will be based in our São Paulo office. We are a hybrid environment that combines the energy and connections of being in the office with the benefits and flexibility of working from home. We currently require a minimum of three coordinated days in the office per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday. As a perk, we also have up to four weeks per year of fully remote work!</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain the release infrastructure that powers Brex’s deployment pipelines and incident workflows</li> <li>Drive technical strategy and architecture for release and observability systems, making them more scalable, reliable, and secure</li> <li>Collaborate with product, engineering, and operations partners to ensure Brex’s releases are safe, predictable, and low-friction</li> <li>Identify and deliver improvements to the end-to-end release process (from code merge to production) to reduce risk and cycle time</li> <li>Build and evolve tooling for observability and incident response, enabling fast detection, triage, and resolution</li> <li>Proactively identify and mitigate risks in our release and infrastructure stack, including performance, reliability, and security concerns</li> <li>Define, instrument, and monitor key metrics for release engineering (e.g., deployment frequency, change failure rate, MTTR) and use them to guide improvements</li> <li>Partner with other infrastructure and product teams to debug complex production issues and drive long-term fixes</li> <li>Contribute to and champion best practices in release engineering, reliability, and operational excellence across the organization</li> <li>Mentor other engineers on the team, providing technical guidance and code reviews to elevate the overall quality of our infrastructure</li> <li>Stay up-to-date on emerging tools and practices in release engineering, observability, and SRE, and bring relevant ideas into Brex’s stack</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of professional experience designing, building, and operating backend or infrastructure systems in production</li> <li>Strong proficiency in backend programming languages (e.g., Go, Java, Kotlin, or Python) with a focus on reliability and performance</li> <li>Hands-on experience with CI/CD and release pipelines (e.g., GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Buildkite, Argo, Spinnaker, Jenkins) including build, test, and deployment automation</li> <li>Experience architecting and operating scalable, high-availability distributed systems on cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure)</li> <li>Deep familiarity with containerization and orchestration (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation)</li> <li>Experience designing and maintaining observability tooling (metrics, logs, tracing) and integrating it into incident response workflows</li> <li>Strong understanding of reliability and SRE practices, including SLIs/SLOs, error budgets, and incident management best practices</li> <li>Experience designing and optimizing data storage systems (SQL and/or NoSQL) for operational and observability use cases</li> <li>Proven track record of improving release processes (e.g., reducing deployment risk, increasing deployment frequency, automating rollbacks)</li> <li>Comfort working cross-functionally with product and other engineering teams to debug complex production issues and ship changes safely</li> <li>Strong communication and collaboration skills, including writing clear design docs and driving technical decisions across teams</li> </ul> <p>Brex LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One, N.A.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Brex recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with a&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://brex.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://brex.com" data-sk="tooltip_parent">brex.com</a>&nbsp;domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Brex via other sources should be ignored.</em></p></div>

title: Software Engineer, Android
description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About PhonePe Limited:</strong></p> <p>Headquartered in India, its flagship product, the PhonePe digital payments app, was launched in Aug 2016. As of April 2025, PhonePe has over 60 Crore (600 Million) registered users and a digital payments acceptance network spread across over 4 Crore (40+ million) merchants. PhonePe also processes over 33 Crore (330+ Million) transactions daily with an Annualized Total Payment Value (TPV) of over INR 150 lakh crore.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>PhonePe’s portfolio of businesses includes the distribution of financial products (Insurance,&nbsp;Lending, and Wealth) as well as new consumer tech businesses (Pincode - hyperlocal e-commerce and Indus AppStore Localized App Store for the Android ecosystem) in India,&nbsp;which are aligned with the company’s vision to offer every Indian an equal opportunity to&nbsp;accelerate their progress by unlocking the flow of money and access to services.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Culture:</strong></p> <p>At PhonePe, we go the extra mile to make sure you can bring your best self to work, Everyday!. And that starts with creating the right environment for you. We empower people and trust them to do the right&nbsp; thing. Here, you own your work from start to finish, right from day one. PhonePe-rs solve complex problems and execute quickly; often building frameworks from scratch. If you’re excited by the idea of building platforms that touch millions, ideating with some of&nbsp; the best minds in the country and executing on your dreams with purpose and speed, join us!</p> <p><video controls="controls" width="300" height="150"> <source src="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mkZstuTlMFTM8sDh1x4NYm7WSLueqklb/view?resourcekey"></video></p></div><p>About PhonePe Limited:</p> <p>At PhonePe, we take extra care to make sure you give your best at work, Everyday! And creating the<br>right environment for you is just one of the things we do. We empower people and trust them to do<br>the right thing. Here, you own your work from start to finish, right from day one. Being enthusiastic<br>about tech is a big part of being at PhonePe. If you like building technology that impacts millions<br>Ideating with some of the best minds in the country and executing on your dreams with purpose and<br>speed, join us!</p> <p><br>Challenges:</p> <p>Building for Scale, Rapid Iterative Development and Customer-centric Product Thinking at each<br>step defines every day for a developer at PhonePe. Though we engineer for a 50million+ strong<br>userbase,we code with every individual user in mind. While we are quick to adopt the latest in<br>Engineering, we care utmost for security, stability, and automation. Apply if you want to experience<br>the best combination of passionate application development and product-driven thinking.<br>Requirements:<br>• Strong passion for Programming in general and Android App development in specific.<br>• Strong problem-solving skills.<br>• Strong system design and architecture skills - specifically for android.<br>• Curiosity to tinker around, explore new paradigms and strong zest for continuous<br>improvement.<br>• Over 5-9 years of full time Android App development experience with strong basics and complete<br>exposure to Android development.<br>• Idea/experience of unit and instrumentation testing in Android E2E App development<br>and/or experience of developing SDKs is good to have.<br>• By using some of the latest advancements in the world of Android such as Kotlin, MVVM,<br>Data Binding, Dagger 2, Live Data, Room, Work Manage.</p> <p><br>We are building India’s Largest Transactional Platform pivoted on Payments!</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>PhonePe Full Time Employee Benefits (Not applicable for Intern or Contract Roles)</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Insurance Benefits - </strong>Medical Insurance, Critical Illness Insurance, Accidental Insurance, Life Insurance</li> <li><strong>Wellness Program -</strong> Employee Assistance Program, Onsite Medical Center, Emergency Support System</li> <li><strong>Parental Support - </strong>Maternity Benefit, Paternity Benefit Program, Adoption Assistance Program, Day-care Support Program</li> <li><strong>Mobility Benefits - </strong>Relocation benefits, Transfer Support Policy, Travel Policy</li> <li><strong>Retirement Benefits - </strong>Employee PF Contribution, Flexible PF Contribution, Gratuity, NPS, Leave Encashment&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Other Benefits - </strong>Higher Education Assistance, Car Lease, Salary Advance Policy</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><em>Our inclusive culture promotes individual expression, creativity, innovation, and achievement and in turn helps us better understand and serve our customers. We see ourselves as a place for intellectual curiosity,&nbsp; ideas and debates, where diverse perspectives lead to deeper understanding and better quality results. PhonePe is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to treating all its employees and job applicants equally; regardless of&nbsp; gender, sexual preference, religion, race, color or disability. If you have a disability or special need that requires assistance or reasonable accommodation, during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, </em></strong><a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc-ETchy2LsQ_DjlNXOUcGfI182DnA533YZTLfVw5TkJH-Stw/viewform"><strong><em>please fill out this form.</em></strong></a></p> <p><strong><em>Read more about PhonePe</em></strong><a href="https://www.phonepe.com/blog/"><strong><em> on our blog</em></strong></a><strong><em>.</em></strong></p> <p><a href="https://www.phonepe.com/blog/life-at-phonepe/"><strong>Life at PhonePe</strong></a></p> <p><a href="https://www.phonepe.com/press/"><strong>PhonePe in the news</strong></a></p></div>
